question: Synonym of OSTENTATIOUS
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option C Solution: Pretty : (of a person, especially a woman or child) attractive in a delicate way without being truly beautiful. Modest : unassuming in the estimation of one's abilities or achievements. Showy : having a striking appearance or style, typically by being excessively bright, colourful, or ostentatious. Flagrant : (of an action considered wrong or immoral) conspicuously or obviously offensive. Ostentatious : characterized by pretentious or showy display; designed to impress. Synonym of Ostentatious is Showy

question: Synonym of DERIDE
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option A Solution: Mock : tease or laugh at in a scornful or contemptuous manner. Confuse : make (someone) bewildered or perplexed. Shout : (of a person) utter a loud cry, typically as an expression of a strong emotion. Snarl : (of an animal such as a dog) make an aggressive growl with bared teeth. Deride : express contempt for; ridicule. Synonym of Deride is Mock

question: Synonym of SCUTTLE
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option B Solution: Pollute : contaminate (water, the air, etc.) with harmful or poisonous substances. Sabotage : deliberately destroy, damage, or obstruct (something), especially for political or military advantage. Shout : (of a person) utter a loud cry, typically as an expression of a strong emotion. Succeed : achieve the desired aim or result. Scuttle : sink (one's own ship) deliberately by holing it or opening its seacocks to let water in. Synonym of Scuttle is Sabotage

question: Synonym of EPHEMERAL
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option C Solution: Unreal : imaginary or illusory. Mythical : occurring in or characteristic of myths or folk tales. Short-lived : living or lasting only a little while. Artificial : made or produced by human beings rather than occurring naturally, especially as a copy of something natural. Ephemeral : lasting for a very short time. Synonym of Ephemeral is Short-lived

question: Synonym of ABRIDGE
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option C Solution: Abort : carry out or undergo the abortion of (a fetus). Span : the full extent of something from end to end; the amount of space that something covers. Shorten : make or become shorter. Cross : a mark, object, or figure formed by two short intersecting lines or pieces (+ or ×). Abridge : shorten (a piece of writing) without losing the sense. Synonym of Abridge is Shorten

question: Synonym of IMMACULATE
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option A Solution: Spotless : absolutely clean or pure; immaculate. Dirty : covered or marked with an unclean substance. Shining : giving out or reflecting bright light. Clean : free from dirt, marks, or stains. Immaculate : perfectly clean, neat, or tidy. Synonym of Immaculate is Spotless

question: Synonym of ACCENTUATED
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option C Solution: Projected : estimate or forecast (something) on the basis of present trends. Exhibited : publicly display (a work of art or item of interest) in an art gallery or museum or at a trade fair. Sharpened : improve or cause to improve. Mitigated : make (something bad) less severe, serious, or painful. Accentuated : make more noticeable or prominent. Synonym of Accentuated is Sharpened

question: Synonym of BUCOLIC
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option D Solution: Helpless : unable to defend oneself or to act without help. Intoxicated : (of alcoholic drink or a drug) cause (someone) to lose control of their faculties or behaviour. Shameless : (of a person or their conduct) characterized by or showing a lack of shame; barefaced or brazen. Rustic : relating to the countryside; rural. Bucolic : relating to the pleasant aspects of the countryside and country life. Synonym of Bucolic is Rustic

question: Synonym of ABSTRUSE
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option D Solution: Awful : very bad or unpleasant. Irrelevant : not connected with or relevant to something. Shallow : of little depth. Profound : (of a subject or idea) demanding deep study or thought. Abstruse : difficult to understand; obscure. Synonym of Abstruse is Profound

question: Synonym of MAIM
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option A Solution: Disfigure : spoil the appearance of. Slit : a long, narrow cut or opening. Severe : (of something bad or undesirable) very great; intense. Slash : cut with a wide, sweeping movement, typically using a knife or sword. Maim : wound or injure (a person or animal) so that part of the body is permanently damaged. Synonym of Maim is Disfigure

question: Synonym of MUTATION
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option A Solution: Variation : a change or slight difference in condition, amount, or level, typically within certain limits. Display : put (something) in a prominent place in order that it may readily be seen. Severance : the action of ending a connection or relationship. Silence : complete absence of sound. Mutation : the changing of the structure of a gene, resulting in a variant form that may be transmitted to subsequent generations, caused by the alteration of single base units in DNA, or the deletion, insertion, or rearrangement of larger sections of genes or chromosomes. Synonym of Mutation is Variation

question: Synonym of MOROSE
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option A Solution: Gloomy : causing or feeling depression or despondency. Stupid : having or showing a great lack of intelligence or common sense. Serious : demanding or characterized by careful consideration or application. Stern : (of a person or their manner) serious and unrelenting, especially in the assertion of authority and exercise of discipline. Morose : sullen and ill-tempered. Synonym of Morose is Gloomy

question: Synonym of LETHARGY
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option D Solution: Laxity : looseness of a limb or muscle. Impassivity : apathy demonstrated by an absence of emotional reactions. Serenity : the state of being calm, peaceful, and untroubled. Listlessness : Lacking energy or disinclined to exert effort. Lethargy : a lack of energy and enthusiasm. Synonym of Lethargy is Listlessness

question: Synonym of OGLE
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option D Solution: Complain : express dissatisfaction or annoyance about something. Mix : combine or put together to form one substance or mass. Separate : forming or viewed as a unit apart or by itself. Stare : look fixedly or vacantly at someone or something with one's eyes wide open. Ogle : stare at in a lecherous manner. Synonym of Ogle is Stare

question: Synonym of RESILIENT
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option A Solution: Flexible : capable of bending easily without breaking. Proud : feeling deep pleasure or satisfaction as a result of one's own achievements, qualities, or possessions or those of someone with whom one is closely associated. Separable : able to be separated or treated separately. Rigid : unable to bend or be forced out of shape; not flexible. Resilient : (of a substance or object) able to recoil or spring back into shape after bending, stretching, or being compressed. Synonym of Resilient is Flexible

question: Synonym of OVERSTRUNG
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option C Solution: Concerned : worried, troubled, or anxious. Active : engaging or ready to engage in physically energetic pursuits. Sensitive : having or displaying a quick and delicate appreciation of others' feelings. Energetic : showing or involving great activity or vitality. Overstrung : (of a person) extremely nervous or tense. Synonym of Overstrung is Sensitive

question: Synonym of GROGGY
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option A Solution: Unsteady : liable to fall or shake; not steady in position. Sensible : done or chosen in accordance with wisdom or prudence; likely to be of benefit. Sensitive : quick to detect or respond to slight changes, signals, or influences. Pensive : engaged in, involving, or reflecting deep or serious thought. Groggy : dazed, weak, or unsteady, especially from illness, intoxication, sleep, or a blow. Synonym of Groggy is Unsteady

question: Synonym of ABNEGATION
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option A Solution: Self-denial : the denial of one's own interests and needs; self-sacrifice. Self-indulgence : something done or allowed in a self-indulgent way. Self-praise : the action of praising oneself; boasting. Self-criticism : criticism of oneself or one's actions. Abnegation : self-denial. Synonym of Abnegation is Self-denial

question: Synonym of INTREPID
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option B Solution: Coward : a person who is contemptibly lacking in the courage to do or endure dangerous or unpleasant things. Fearless : showing a lack of fear. Selfish : (of a person, action, or motive) lacking consideration for other people; concerned chiefly with one's own personal profit or pleasure. Ugly : unpleasant or repulsive, especially in appearance. Intrepid : fearless; adventurous (often used for rhetorical or humorous effect). Synonym of Intrepid is Fearless

question: Synonym of CAUTIOUSLY
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option B Solution: Genuinely : in a truthful way. Carefully : in a way that deliberately avoids harm or errors; cautiously. Secretly : in a secret way; without others knowing. Somewhat : to a moderate extent or by a moderate amount; rather. Cautiously : in a way that deliberately avoids potential problems or dangers. Synonym of Cautiously is Carefully

question: Synonym of SURREPTITIOUS
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option D Solution: Mysterious : difficult or impossible to understand, explain, or identify. Abstract : existing in thought or as an idea but not having a physical or concrete existence. Open : allowing access, passage, or a view through an empty space; not closed or blocked. Secret : not known or seen or not meant to be known or seen by others. Surreptitious : kept secret, especially because it would not be approved of. Synonym of Surreptitious is Secret

question: Synonym of ECONOMISE
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option C Solution: Accumulate : gather together or acquire an increasing number or quantity of. Minimise : reduce (something, especially something undesirable) to the smallest possible amount or degree. Save : keep and store up (something, especially money) for future use. Reduce : make smaller or less in amount, degree, or size. Economise : spend less; reduce one's expenses. Synonym of Economise is Save

question: Synonym of SAGACITY
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option B Solution: Morality : principles concerning the distinction between right and wrong or good and bad behaviour. Wisdom : the quality of having experience, knowledge, and good judgement; the quality of being wise. Sanity : the ability to think and behave in a normal and rational manner; sound mental health. Uprightness : the condition or quality of being honourable or honest; rectitude. Sagacity : the quality of being sagacious; wisdom. Synonym of Sagacity is Wisdom

question: Synonym of SABULOUS
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option C Solution: Splotched : make a daub, blot, or smear on. Itching : be the site of or cause an itch. Sandy : covered in or consisting mostly of sand. Furry : covered with fur. Sabulous : being sandy or gritty. Synonym of Sabulous is Sandy

question: Synonym of SECURE
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option C Solution: Secret : not known or seen or not meant to be known or seen by others. Comfortable : (especially of clothes or furnishings) providing physical ease and relaxation. Safe : protected from or not exposed to danger or risk; not likely to be harmed or lost. Independent : free from outside control; not subject to another's authority. Secure : fixed or fastened so as not to give way, become loose, or be lost. Synonym of Secure is Safe
